title: podman machine init --provider wsl fails on Windows without HyperV enabled and user is member of HyperV admins (6.0.0)
body:
### Issue Description

On Windows 11 Pro with WSL2 installed and HyperV disabled trying to run
`podman machine init --provider wsl`
returns
`Error: HyperV namespace not found, is HyperV enabled?`
Podman version 6.0.0
On same system, with same settings with podman 5.8.3 command works.
When HyperV feature is enabled (podman 6.0.0) it also works.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Windows 11 Pro host with WSL2 installed and HyperV disabled
2. Run podman machine init --provider wsl


### Describe the results you received

Command aborts
```
time="…" level=debug msg="Using Podman machine with `wsl` virtualization provider"
time="…" level=debug msg="socket length for C:\\Users\\[user]\\.config\\containers\\podman\\machine\\wsl is 53"
time="…" level=debug msg="socket length for C:\\Users\\[user]\\.local\\share\\containers\\podman\\machine\\wsl is 58"
time="…" level=debug msg="socket length for C:\\Users\\[user]\\.local\\share\\containers\\podman\\machine\\wsl\\cache is 64"
time="…" level=debug msg="socket length for C:\\Users\\[user]\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\podman is 41"
time="…" level=debug msg="socket length for C:\\Users\\[user]\\.config\\containers\\podman\\machine\\wsl\\podman-machine-default.json is 81"
time="…" level=debug msg="socket length for C:\\Users\\[user]\\.config\\containers\\podman\\machine\\hyperv is 56"
time="…" level=debug msg="socket length for C:\\Users\\[user]\\.local\\share\\containers\\podman\\machine\\hyperv is 61"
time="…" level=debug msg="socket length for C:\\Users\\[user]\\.local\\share\\containers\\podman\\machine\\hyperv\\cache is 67"
time="…" level=debug msg="socket length for C:\\Users\\[user]\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\podman is 41"
Error: HyperV namespace not found, is HyperV enabled?
time="…" level=debug msg="Shutting down engines"
```

### Describe the results you expected

Podman should have created podman machine with wsl provider without HyperV enabled.

### podman info output

```yaml
Client:
  APIVersion: 6.0.0
  Built: 1782322337
  BuiltTime: Wed Jun 24 19:32:17 2026
  GitCommit: a8ed4b6dd12992decf659cadfdfb3d0cb1937748
  GoVersion: go1.26.4
  Os: windows
  OsArch: windows/amd64
  Version: 6.0.0
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

```
OS Name:                       Microsoft Windows 11 Pro
OS Version:                    10.0.26200 N/A Build 26200
OS Manufacturer:               Microsoft Corporation
OS Configuration:              Standalone Workstation
OS Build Type:                 Multiprocessor Free
System Type:                   x64-based PC
```

### Additional information

machine init probably fails when checking something on the hypervisor exists with the same name 
[cmd/podman/machine/init.go#L227](https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/blob/a8ed4b6dd12992decf659cadfdfb3d0cb1937748/cmd/podman/machine/init.go#L227)
but since HyperV is not enabled it returns error.

Issue #29053 is not related since it affects host with HyperV enabled and WSL not installed.

comments.body:
@wwasko thank you for reporting this. It's definitely a bug. To evaluate the severirty can you confirm that either:

- You have executed `podman machine init` from an administrator terminal
   **OR**
- Your user is a member of the Hyper-V admin groups (`Get-LocalGroupMember -Name "Hyper-V Administrators"`)

In other words: `machine init` should work, even with Hyper-V disabled, when executed from a non-admin terminal and the user isn't a member of the HyperV admins group

> [@l0rd](https://github.com/l0rd) I have checked:
> 
> * `podman machine init` doesn't work from administrator terminal
> * user is a member of Hyper-V admin group

Thank you for confirming. Your use case is not a common one (user is an Hyper-V admin but Hyper-V is disabled) and I have updated the issue title to reflect that. Anyway it's still a bug and I have submitted a [PR to fix it](https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/pull/29142).

cc @lstocchi
